<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Serpent Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4609)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glistening oil has a green hue and tiny snake scales floating within. If you slather _serpent oil_ on a Tiny object that is snakelike in shape, from a stick to a scarf, the object transforms into a viper, keeping some of the same colors and patterns of the original item. If placed on other objects, the oil fails and is wasted. This false snake has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait. It remains in snake form for 1 minute before returning to its object state. If slain, the item returns to its original form, unharmed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

The oil can be applied to a Small object, producing a [sea snake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=807), or a Medium object, producing a [ball python](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3201).

---

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Weapon-Weird Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4610)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 87

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each dose of _weapon-weird oil_ is keyed to a particular melee weapon group, selected from among [axe](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1), [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4), [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5), [flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7), [hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8), [knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9), [pick](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=10), [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11), [shield](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12), [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14), and [sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15). The oil creates a synergy between skill and weapon, enabling you to wield the weapon in unexpected ways. You must have proficiency with the original weapon to benefit from the oil; however, you use your proficiency rank with the oil’s keyed group instead of the weapon’s original group. Also, you apply the critical specialization effect from the oil’s keyed group instead of the weapon’s normal critical specialization effect. While the oil remains effective, the grievous rune and similar magic react as if the weapon belongs to the oil’s group. A weapon can be coated in only one type of weapon-weird oil at a time. Any new application of this oil supersedes any previous one. These effects last for 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Weapon-Weird Oil

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wounding Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4611)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 87

**Price** 45 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Smearing _wounding oil_, a crimson fluid, on a weapon causes it to smell strongly of blood and gives it the benefits of the [_wounding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) rune for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wounding Oil

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 88 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- _Cayden’s brew_ is like rich beer or ale, with a golden-brown color and foamy head. For 1 hour after you drink it, you have a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effects. Also, you can use a single action to breathe out a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of intoxicating vapor with a burp that can be heard for 100 feet. Any creature in the vapor must attempt a DC 25 Fortitude saving throw. After you use this breath weapon, you can’t do so again for 1d4 rounds. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is clumsy 1 and stupefied 1 for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature becomes clumsy 2, stupefied 2, and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. The clumsy and stupefied conditions last until 1d4 rounds after the sickened condition ends. Cayden's Brew **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 88 **Price** 80 gp Cayden's Brew (Double) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 88 **Price** 480 gp --- The save DC is 31. Cayden's Brew (Triple) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 88 **Price** 3,200 gp --- The save DC is 40.
name: Cayden's Brew

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cloning Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4613)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 88

**Price** 5,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you drink a _cloning potion_, you split in two, one version of you remaining in your space and the other moving into an adjacent space. Your clone, which has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait, looks like you and remains for 1 minute. Provided you are both on the same plane, you can command your clone telepathically with a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. You can also issue verbal commands, as normal for a minion. As an action that has the concentrate trait, you can sense through your clone. When you do, you lose all sensory information from your own body. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this sense-sharing effect. 

You and your clone share Hit Points, and the clone uses your statistics. Spells and effects target you and your clone as if you are separate creatures. The clone can Activate your abilities and Cast your Spells, limited by its number of actions, but you share elements such as use limits, frequency, and spell slots. In other words, your clone using one of your resources works as if you did so in or from your clone’s space. Your clone has gear identical to your own, excepting artifacts and consumables. Any item taken from the clone melts into silvery dust within seconds, with ammunition or thrown objects lasting just long enough to travel to and possibly hit their target.

When the duration lapses, your clone melts into silvery dust. You become temporarily immune to _cloning potion_ for 1d4 days

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cloning Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eidetic Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4614)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 89

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Bright pink, pale lavender, and vibrant orange colors swirl through this purplish liquid, making the _eidetic potion_ resemble an unforgettable sunset. When you drink this potion, for 1 round, anything you observe becomes locked into your memory. You can recall the memory perfectly and gain a +2 status bonus to create representations of that memory, whether using [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) to create an artistic rendition or [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) to create a Forgery. The memory remains locked for 1 week or until you benefit from the bonus it imparts for the first time, whichever comes first; afterward, it becomes a normal, fallible memory. You can only have a single memory locked in your mind at a time. Using this potion while you have a locked memory from a previous use makes the previously locked memory fallible but allows you to lock a new memory.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eidetic Potion

>
[Elysian Dew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4615)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 89

**Price** 375 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you drink sweet, sky-blue _Elysian dew_, for 1 minute, you gain a 10-foot aura that evokes the vitality of [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40), causing nearby objects to seem more colorful and plants to stand taller. You and any ally that starts its turn in the emanation gain 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321), a +1 item bonus to saving throws, and a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks until the start of your or the ally’s next turn. If you’re [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) and drink this potion, it fails to work, and you must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or the potion renders you [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Elysian Dew

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Empath's Cordial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4616)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 89

**Price** 65 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The pale liquid of _empath’s cordial_ changes color to reflect the mood of the creature nearest to it—turning blue for calm, red for anger, and green for envious, among other hues. For 1 hour after you drink this potion, you can sense the presence of general emotions, such as hostility toward you or the presence of an emotion effect impacting a creature’s emotions, within 30 feet. A creature that failed a saving throw against [_calm emotions_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) can’t be detected. This potion doesn’t allow you to automatically tell what emotions a specific creature is experiencing, but you can attempt a [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) check with a +2 item bonus (DC set by the GM) to discern that information. The potion also grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) against creatures whose emotions you can sense.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Empath's Cordial

    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Enigma-Sight Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4617)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Revelation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=138" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 89

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A vial of this _enigma-sight potion_ seems to contain fragments of kaleidoscopic crystal that reflect mind-bending scenes. However, these “crystals” act like liquid when poured or consumed. Drinking the potion grants you the effects of a [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) spell, except that you use your [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) modifier and your level for the secret [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check instead of the normal counteract modifier and spell level. However, if you critically fail the secret counteract check the effect grants, your mind fills in false, nightmarish information. You become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1d4 rounds and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 as your brain struggles to process what you see.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Enigma-Sight Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hovering Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4619)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 90

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A foamy, cloudy liquid, _hovering potion_ enables you to defy gravity for 5 minutes. By default, the potion causes you to float several inches off the ground. If you take a [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) action, you can move 10 feet up or down in the air. You can instead [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) at your full Speed or Stride to move along a horizontal surface at half your Speed. The GM determines which surfaces can be climbed or moved across this way. If you fall while this potion is in effect, you can use the [Arrest a Fall](/Actions.aspx?ID=2308) reaction as if you had a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350). If you have a fly Speed, you remain airborne at the end of your turn, even if you didn’t use a Fly action this round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hovering Potion

When you drink the thick, slate-colored _immovable potion_, you become anchored in place, even defying gravity, rendering you [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 minute or until you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the activation. While you are immobilized this way, the DC to move you from your place, including knocking you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), is 40.

---

Small bolts of brightly colored electricity flicker through the cloudy _mind-swap potion_. The potion often comes in a double‑chambered flask, because when you drink it, you consume half the contents. If another creature of the same ancestry consumes the remainder of the contents within 1 minute, your minds swap per the effect of a critical success on a [_mind swap_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=61) ritual. The effects last for 24 hours or until one of you Dismisses the activation.

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

---

Quenching potion is a clear liquid that tastes like fresh, cool spring water. Drinking this potion or pouring it over yourself completely hydrates you and cleanses your system. Immediately attempt a [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to end any [persistent acid, fire, negative, or poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) affecting you, and attempt a new saving throw against any poison affecting you. Poison can’t progress to a worse stage due to this saving throw. The potion counts as a particularly appropriate type of help against persistent acid, fire, negative, and poison damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rebirth Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4623)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 90

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A small bit from a humanoid—such as a hair, scale, or feather— steeps in the clear liquid of a _rebirth potion_. When the potion is created, this bit determines the ancestry and heritage the potion changes the imbiber to. After you drink the potion, you transform into that ancestry over 8 hours during your next period of rest, finishing the transformation after the 8 hours are up. Throughout this time, you are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2, [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2, and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2. Once the transformation is complete, the potion’s magic ends and can’t be counteracted. Replace your ancestry Hit Points, size, Speeds, ability boosts, ability flaws, traits, and special abilities with those of your new ancestry. You lose your ancestry feats, selecting replacements valid for your new ancestry. You have mild control over the change, but you end up with a unique appearance fitting for your new ancestry, and some quirks of your body remain, such as relative age, general health, scars, and missing digits, limbs, or organs.

Drinking a _rebirth potion_ of your current ancestry works normally, allowing you to rearrange some of the cited ancestry elements and change your appearance (provided you abide by the potion’s limitations regarding health and age).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rebirth Potion

>
[Rovagug's Mud](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4625)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

---

_Rovagug’s mud_ smells and tastes like wet, sour earth. For 1 hour after you drink it, you have a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effects. Also, you can use a single action to breathe out a 20-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of bitter vapor that causes an earth-shaking rumble that can be heard for 100 feet. Creatures and objects in the area take 5d6 damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save), decreasing Hardness by 5. An object’s Hardness remains lowered for 1d4 rounds, and you can’t use this breath again for the same amount of time.

>
[Rovagug's Mud](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4625)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />

<column gap="tiny">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

---

_Rovagug’s mud_ smells and tastes like wet, sour earth. For 1 hour after you drink it, you have a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effects. Also, you can use a single action to breathe out a 20-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of bitter vapor that causes an earth-shaking rumble that can be heard for 100 feet. Creatures and objects in the area take 5d6 damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save), decreasing Hardness by 5. An object’s Hardness remains lowered for 1d4 rounds, and you can’t use this breath again for the same amount of time.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Starsong Nectar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4626)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 91

**Price** 14,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A liquid with the appearance of an expressive night sky and a taste that’s sheer pleasure, _starsong nectar_ grants you cheerful confidence and incredible luck for 10 minutes after you drink it. However, if you show too much caution at any point during this time (GM’s discretion), you must succeed at a DC 6 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or the potion’s effects end. While the potion lasts, you gain a +3 status bonus to attack rolls, initiative rolls, Perception checks, saving throws, and skill checks, and you aren’t [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) creatures or when [flanked](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375). You are temporarily immune to this potion for 24 hours once its effects end.

While the potion lasts, though, if you take a moment to imagine the future, you choose the best courses of action. This aspect of the potion grants you the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** Until the start of your next turn, roll two d20s for any attack rolls, Perception checks, saving throws, and skill checks you make and take the higher result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Starsong Nectar

    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sure-Step Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4627)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 91

**Price** 17 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The light-brown liquid of a _sure-step potion_ helps you find your footing. After drinking it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) for 1 hour. In addition, you can [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) into [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366), and you aren’t [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) on [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sure-Step Potion

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tentacle Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4628)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 91

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Upon consuming this mottled, foul-tasting liquid, the _tentacle potion_ causes you to extrude a long, flexible limb of ectoplasm. Your clothing doesn’t need to accommodate this limb of ghostly matter, which can extrude through your clothing and armor. The limb lasts 1 hour, and you can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the activation. You can’t hide or disguise the tentacle. You can use the limb to perform simple [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions, such as opening an unlocked door. Your limb can’t perform actions that require significant manual dexterity, including any action that would require a check to accomplish. You can’t use it to hold items. At one time, you can have only one extra limb from any version of this potion. Stronger _tentacle potions_ replace the effects of weaker ones.

If you have the [Flexible Tail](/Feats.aspx?ID=5639) or [Skillful Tail](/Feats.aspx?ID=4557) feats, the [Tailed Goblin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=37) heritage, or a similar feature the GM believes would benefit, this potion can instead fortify your tail. A fortified tail benefits from any _tentacle potion_ as if the potion were the next better type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tentacle Potion (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 91

**Price** 33 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tentacle Potion (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 91

**Price** 155 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

As lesser, but you can use the limb to hold an item of up to light Bulk. You can also use the whole limb to hold onto a suitable anchor point, such as a tree branch, balcony, or rocky outcropping, subject to the GM’s discretion. While using your limb this way, you have free use of all your other limbs, so you can perform tasks that require both hands. This potion lasts 8 hours

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tentacle Potion (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 91

**Price** 660 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

As moderate, but the tail can hold an item of up to 1 Bulk or be used as a second hand to help hold an item of up to 2 Bulk. You can also use the limb to make an unarmed attack that deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage and has the sweep trait. This potion lasts 24 hours.
name: Tentacle Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tentacle Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4628)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 91

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Upon consuming this mottled, foul-tasting liquid, the _tentacle potion_ causes you to extrude a long, flexible limb of ectoplasm. Your clothing doesn’t need to accommodate this limb of ghostly matter, which can extrude through your clothing and armor. The limb lasts 1 hour, and you can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the activation. You can’t hide or disguise the tentacle. You can use the limb to perform simple [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions, such as opening an unlocked door. Your limb can’t perform actions that require significant manual dexterity, including any action that would require a check to accomplish. You can’t use it to hold items. At one time, you can have only one extra limb from any version of this potion. Stronger _tentacle potions_ replace the effects of weaker ones.

If you have the [Flexible Tail](/Feats.aspx?ID=5639) or [Skillful Tail](/Feats.aspx?ID=4557) feats, the [Tailed Goblin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=37) heritage, or a similar feature the GM believes would benefit, this potion can instead fortify your tail. A fortified tail benefits from any _tentacle potion_ as if the potion were the next better type.

<column gap="tiny">

    level="1"

>
[Amnemonic Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4629)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 92

**Price** 140 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You fail or critically fail a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394), [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389), [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393)

**Requirement** You’re a master in the skill you failed with.
</row>

</column>

---

This ornate locket, usually containing a faceless, humanoid portrait, is typically worn on a chain around the neck or attached to your clothing. When activated, the charm casts a DC 26 [_modify memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) spell against a single target of the check, removing all memory of your failed attempt and potentially allowing you to make another attempt. The effect can’t remove more than 3 minutes of memories from its target

</column>

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Trigger** You roll initiative using [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) and can see a creature

**Requirement** You are a master in Stealth
</row>

</column>

---

This small, matte-black pin always seems to be on the periphery of your vision, even when you stare directly at it. When you Activate the talisman, choose one creature you can see. You become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) to that creature unless it succeeds at a DC 28 Will save. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until the target hits you with an attack, whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Captivating Bauble](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4631)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 92

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You are a master in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39)
</row>

</column>

---

This talisman appears as an ornate piece of jewelry of the highest quality. When you Activate it, your speech and mannerisms become supernaturally compelling for up to 1 hour. By engaging an intelligent creature in conversation for at least 1 minute, you can cause them to become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) unless they succeed at a DC 30 Will save. This fascination lasts for as long as you continue conversing or until you move at least 20 feet away. When the effect ends, the target becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours. If you or any ally within 120 feet takes an overtly hostile action while a creature is fascinated by the bauble, the bauble burns out in a shower of sparks and all its effects end.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Captivating Bauble
requirement_markdown: You are a master in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39)

[Chain of Stars](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4632)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 92

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to thrown weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You hit a creature with the affixed weapon.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This delicate [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2917) chain strung with tiny shuriken is wound tightly around the handle of the affixed weapon. When you Activate the chain, three _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [shuriken](/Weapons.aspx?ID=439)_ made from magical force materialize in the target’s space and split off to attack other creatures. Attempt up to three shuriken [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) that must each target a different creature and can’t target the creature you hit to trigger this talisman. These Strikes use your attack modifier but originate from the hit creature’s space. These attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn’t increase until after all three attacks have been made. The shuriken deal [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) damage instead of their normal type, and each shuriken vanishes after its attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Chain of Stars

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragonclaw Scutcheon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4634)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 92

**Price** 1,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You would take damage of a type depending on the talisman’s dragon type: [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) damage

 
</row>

</column>

---

This decorative shield emblem contains the gilded claw of a dragon mounted in a setting of high-grade [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) alloy. It protects against a damage type depending on the type of dragon the claw came from (see the sidebar). When you Activate the scutcheon, you and all of your carried, wielded, or worn items gain immunity to all damage of that type until the end of your next turn.

If you have a _dragonclaw scutcheon_, [_dragonscale cameo_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2102), and _[dragontooth trophy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2103)_ attached to your items, and they all correspond to the same type of dragon, you gain an extra benefit. When you Activate any of these talismans, you gain the ability to cast an 8th-level, DC 35 [_dragon breath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2103) focus spell as an [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2232) once before the end of your next turn. Use the dragon type that matches the talismans; you can Cast the Spell this way without spending a Focus Point. Because each talisman disintegrates when used, you can’t get this benefit again until you’ve attached a replacement.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one claw from an adult or older dragon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragonclaw Scutcheon

<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 93

**Price** 400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ornamental pin, consisting of a single preserved [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395) scale from a [chromatic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=34) or [metallic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=35) dragon mounted in a setting of precious metal, is typically affixed between the shoulder blades. When you Activate the pin, a pair of draconic wings matching the color of the scale unfurl from your shoulders, granting you a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 50 feet for 5 minutes.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one scale from an adult or older dragon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragonscale Cameo

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragontooth Trophy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4636)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 93

**Price** 800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You succeed at a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the affixed weapon

 
</row>

</column>

---

This imposing fang is engraved with an intricate arrangement of symbols, glyphs, and patterns and dangles from a leather strap bound to the hilt of the affixed weapon. When you Activate the trophy, your weapon is momentarily transformed into a magical construct of draconic fury. On the triggering Strike and until the end of your next turn, the damage type of the affixed weapon changes to the type matching the dragon the claw came from (see the sidebar). This change overrides the [versatile](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) trait and similar abilities.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one tooth from an adult or older [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragontooth Trophy

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Emergency Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4637)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 93

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59), or an effect otherwise impedes your vision

**Requirement** You are an expert in [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040).
</row>

</column>

---

This eye, harvested from a monster, looks like it’s peacefully sleeping but gives you a backup when you can’t use your eyes. When the eye is activated, the eyelid pops open, and the eye stares frantically about. You see through the eye with normal vision until the end of your next turn, when the eye withers and flakes away.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Emergency Eye

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ensnaring Disk](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4638)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 93

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You use the affixed shield to [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) a melee weapon attack

**Requirement** You are an expert in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36).
</row>

</column>

---

This coin-sized metal disk is inscribed with arcane symbols and mounted on the inner surface of a shield. When you Activate the disk, the triggering weapon momentarily sticks to your shield, allowing you to attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) it from its wielder with a +2 item bonus. If you roll a critical failure on this check, you get a failure instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ensnaring Disk

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[False Death Vial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4639)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 93

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to  explorer’s clothing  (or another item in the unarmored defense category

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You would be reduced to 0 Hit Points by damage but not immediately killed

**Requirement** You are unarmored.
</row>

</column>

---

This tiny crystal vial of oily liquid is typically attached to a pin or worn on a tether attached to the affixed article of clothing. When you Activate the vial, you avoid being knocked out and remain at 1 Hit Point, your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition increases by 1, and the talisman casts drop dead on you. You and any items you’re wearing and holding are instantly transported from your current space to a clear space of your choice within 30 feet. You take no damage from the triggering effect. If you are carrying another creature (even one contained inside an extradimensional container), it’s left behind in your original space.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
False Death Vial
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 94

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
False Death Vial (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 94

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The talisman teleports you to a clear space of your choice that you can see within 120 feet. This also transports any items you’re wearing and holding with you, as well as up to one other creature currently in contact with you—including if you’re carrying it in an extradimensional container. This effect has the [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) trait.
name: False Death Vial

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hungering Maw](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4642)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 94

**Price** 2,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You attempt a saving throw against a [possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=671) effect

**Requirement** You are a master in Will saves.
</row>

</column>

---

A shining onyx gemstone clutched in a pair of black steel jaws, this pendant is a potent defense against hostile spirits and other beings who commandeer the bodies of others. When you Activate it, you gain a +4 item bonus to your saving throw against the triggering effect. If your result prevents you from being [possessed](/Traits.aspx?ID=671), the creature that attempted to possess you is subject to a DC 37 [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664) spell, which can trap it in the talisman as if it had recently died. The talisman’s magic is exhausted after use, but a soul trapped this way remains imprisoned inside, as detailed in the spell description. Starting at the end of its next turn, the trapped creature gets a new save to end the effect at the end of each of its turns.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hungering Maw
requirement_markdown: You are a master in Will saves.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 94

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You are reduced to 0 Hit Points by damage but not immediately killed

**Requirement** You are an expert in Fortitude saves.
</row>

</column>

---

This small golden coin is usually stamped with the image of a [boar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2854) or other resilient creature. If the triggering damage would cause you to become [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) 2 (typically due to a critical hit or your critical failure), you become dying 1 instead. In addition, for 10 minutes, you die from the dying condition at dying 5, rather than dying 4.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Reflecting Shard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4644)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 94

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Reaction" /><row>command</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You are targeted by a spell of 5th level or lower

**Requirement** You’re a master in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36), and you have the affixed shield raised.
</row>

</column>

---

This mirrored metal fragment is bolted or welded to the face of the affixed shield. When you Activate it, you attempt to reflect the triggering spell back at its caster with [_spell turning_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2027), using your [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) modifier for the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check. The talisman’s counteract level is 7th.

</column>

</title>

</traits>

</row>

</column>

---

The talisman can reflect spells of any level, and its counteract level is 9th.

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You use the affixed shield to S[hield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) a melee attack

**Requirement** You’re a master in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36), and you have the affixed shield raised.
</row>

</column>

---

This heavy metal emblem is mounted on the face of the affixed shield and has a dull gray gemstone embedded at its center. When you Activate the talisman, the gem shatters, releasing a powerful shockwave in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) that must include the enemy who struck the triggering blow, if possible. Each creature in the cone takes 2d6 sonic damage with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. Any creature that fails its save is pushed 5 feet away from you (or 10 feet on a critical failure).

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reverberating Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4645)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 94

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You use the affixed shield to S[hield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) a melee attack

**Requirement** You’re a master in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36), and you have the affixed shield raised.
</row>

</column>

---

This heavy metal emblem is mounted on the face of the affixed shield and has a dull gray gemstone embedded at its center. When you Activate the talisman, the gem shatters, releasing a powerful shockwave in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) that must include the enemy who struck the triggering blow, if possible. Each creature in the cone takes 2d6 sonic damage with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. Any creature that fails its save is pushed 5 feet away from you (or 10 feet on a critical failure).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Reverberating Stone
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 95

**Price** 275 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Reverberating Stone (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 95

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) is 30 feet, the damage is 3d6, and the DC is 34.
name: Reverberating Stone

    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ruby Capacitor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4646)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 95

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a magical staff;

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You prepared the staff.
</row>

</column>

---

This cluster of unworked ruby is bound to the top of a staff by silver wire. When activated, it adds one temporary charge to the staff. Any spells cast from the staff before the end of the turn expend these charges first. Any unused temporary charges are lost at the end of your turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

**Price** 800 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The number of temporary charges increases to 5.

    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wooden Nickel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4648)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 95

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rough wooden coin hangs from a string drilled through a hole at its center. When you Activate the _wooden nickel_, for 10 minutes, you can cause any object you touch of 2 Bulk or less to look, feel, and smell like valuables of a similar size crafted from a precious metal of your choice. 

A creature that touches or Interacts with an affected object can attempt a [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) check against your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) DC to disbelieve the illusion, and a successful check against your Deception DC with [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) or an appropriate [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill will also reveal the item’s true nature. After 10 minutes have elapsed since you Activated the _wooden nickel_, all affected objects revert to their true form.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wooden Nickel

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bargainer's Instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4649)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 96

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>1 hour (envision, Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _bargainer’s instrument_ is a musical implement. The most typical version is a violin made from maple and spruce, its body stained so deep a purple it appears almost black. When activated, the instrument casts a planar binding ritual to conjure a willing extraplanar talent to compete with you in performance.

The contest could be a musical battle, a dance-off, an oratorical debate—the two of you could even use entirely different types of performance. The loser of the bout is magically bound to perform a task of the winner’s choice within 1 week. The contest consists of taking turns to [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401), starting with your opponent. The [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check’s DC is the higher of the opponent’s [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) DC or Will DC, though the two parties can mutually agree to use an impartial judge instead. The first to reach four successes wins, with each critical successes counting as two successes. If you play the bargainer’s instrument as part of your performance, it grants you a +2 item bonus to the Performance checks.

After the contest, the conjured creature can choose to return to where it came from or remain where you conjured it. The loser must endeavor to complete the task by all reasonable means within 1 week. Failing to complete it is shameful but has no further consequences. The instrument loses its magic and remains a non-magical virtuoso instrument.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bargainer's Instrument

Whispers of gibberish arise from it, making their way to nearby ears despite any other sounds in the area. You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target’s AC. This spellgun has a range increment of 15 feet. Once fired, the spellgun twists in your hand and melts away. **Critical Success** The target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute.
**Success** The target is confused for 1 minute but can attempt a DC 25 Will save at the end of each of its turns to end the effect.
name: Bewildering Spellgun

An _energizing treat_ is a treat made from meat or grains. When you feed your animal companion or familiar an energizing treat, it’s [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 minute. It can use the extra action each round only for [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167), [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), and [Support](/Actions.aspx?ID=2665) actions, and it can do so only if it normally has those actions available and you take the proper action to command it.

When you feed the morsel to your familiar, it gains that familiar ability for 1 hour. If your familiar doesn’t meet the requirements, or if it already has an ability from a _familiar morsel_, the morsel is nothing more than a pleasing snack, its magic wasted.

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 97 **Price** 325 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**10 minutes (envision, Interact) --- _Marvelous pigment_ comes in a small container of shifting, multicolored paint that can magically create an object painted with it. The pigment is applied with a stick tipped with bristles, hair, or fur. The paint flows as you Activate the marvelous pigment and changes color at your whim as you paint. The paint can cover a 10-foot-square, two-dimensional surface. When you’re done painting, attempt a DC 30 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check. **Critical Success** The object you painted emerges from the surface as a real, permanent object no larger than 10 feet in any dimension. It’s non-magical and inanimate and has no value except to certain art collectors. If you paint a creature, machine, or form of energy (such as a campfire), these depictions appear only as inanimate sculptures. The created object has a painterly appearance that clearly marks its unusual origin, but is as real as any other.
**Success** As critical success, but the object constantly sheds flakes of paint until it falls apart, destroyed, after 24 hours.
**Failure** The pigments leave a painting that doesn’t quite look right and doesn’t become an object.
**Critical Failure** Is it a dog? Whatever you made is a pretty lousy painting and doesn’t resemble what you set out to make.
name: Marvelous Pigment

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 97 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact, Strike --- Elegant silver filigree contains the body of this ephemeral item, which is made of solid light. Its shape resembles a pistol, and it’s often carried by hunters of [werecreatures](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=484), [vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480), and [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=389). You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target’s AC. This spellgun has a range increment of 30 feet. The spellgun emits a silvery ray of pure moonlight that deals fire damage depending on its type. The spellgun’s damage is treated as [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. **Critical Success** The target takes double damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the start of your next turn. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Neophyte's Fipple](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4655)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 97

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>[Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401)</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You are untrained in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45).
</row>

</column>

---

Made of polished wood, a _neophyte’s fipple_ is a block flute enchanted to guarantee melodic sound. When you [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401) a song on the fipple to Activate it, your ability modifier, proficiency bonus, and item bonus for the [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check total +7, regardless of what they would normally be. Add other bonuses and penalties to the check normally. Once the magic is used, the fipple remains as a mundane instrument.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Neophyte's Fipple

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Potion Patch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4656)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 97

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You must have the potion patch affixed to your skin.
</row>

</column>

---

A _potion patch_ is a sticky, bandage-like pad that can be filled with one potion and affixed to the skin. Filling the patch and affixing it is a 1-minute activity that takes two hands and has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. A patch has a maximum level of potion it can absorb, depending on the patch’s type. When you Activate the patch, the potion affects you without you needing to have the potion in your hand. The patch’s magic is negated after it’s used, the next time you make your daily preparations, or when another _potion patch_ is affixed to you, whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

**Price** 20 gp

</row>

</column>

---

The potion must be 10th level or lower.

right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The potion must be 15th level or lower.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Potion Patch (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 97

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The potion can be any level.
name: Potion Patch
requirement_markdown: You must have the potion patch affixed to your skin.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Potion Patch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4656)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 97

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You must have the potion patch affixed to your skin.
</row>

</column>

---

A _potion patch_ is a sticky, bandage-like pad that can be filled with one potion and affixed to the skin. Filling the patch and affixing it is a 1-minute activity that takes two hands and has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. A patch has a maximum level of potion it can absorb, depending on the patch’s type. When you Activate the patch, the potion affects you without you needing to have the potion in your hand. The patch’s magic is negated after it’s used, the next time you make your daily preparations, or when another _potion patch_ is affixed to you, whichever comes first.

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 97 **Price** 600 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact, Strike --- A rod of blackened bone with a bulb at one end comprises a _reaper’s spellgun_, which feels heavier than it looks. You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target’s AC. This spellgun has a range increment of 30 feet. The spellgun fires a shadowy ray, then dissolves into gray mist. **Critical Success** The target takes 4d6 negative damage and is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2 and [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1. If it had fewer than half its maximum Hit Points after taking the negative damage, it’s drained 3 instead. If the Hit Point loss from being drained drops the creature to 0 Hit Points, it dies.
**Success** As critical success, but for the drained condition, the target is drained 1, or drained 2 if it had fewer than half its maximum HP.
name: Reaper's Spellgun

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact, Strike --- A broad wooden tube with a handle, a _sparking spellgun_ radiates warmth. You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target’s AC. This spellgun has a range increment of 30 feet. The spellgun fires a small ball of sparks and fire, then crumbles to ash. The ball explodes in a flash when it hits, dealing fire damage and [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) according to its type. **Critical Success** The target takes double damage, takes double persistent damage, is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round, and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) while the persistent damage lasts.
**Success** The target takes full damage, full persistent damage, and is dazzled while the persistent damage lasts. Sparkling Spellgun (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98 **Price** 30 gp --- The damage is 4d6, and the persistent damage is 1d4. Sparkling Spellgun (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98 **Price** 150 gp --- The damage is 7d6, and the persistent damage is 3d4. Sparkling Spellgun (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98 **Price** 600 gp --- The damage is 10d6, and the persistent damage is 5d4.
name: Sparking Spellgun

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spiritual Warhorn](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4659)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _spiritual warhorn_ is a trumpet made of horn, leather, and metal. When you play a single, long note from the warhorn, it calls forth a number of Medium spiritual manifestations of warriors to aid you, according to the horn’s type. Each warrior appears in an open square adjacent to an enemy within 60 feet of you, makes a Strike for 2d6 force damage (with an attack bonus determined by the warhorn’s type), and then disappears. The warriors can [flank](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) with one another and with you and your allies.

Once the magic is used, the warhorn remains as a non‑magical musical instrument.

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _spiritual warhorn_ is a trumpet made of horn, leather, and metal. When you play a single, long note from the warhorn, it calls forth a number of Medium spiritual manifestations of warriors to aid you, according to the horn’s type. Each warrior appears in an open square adjacent to an enemy within 60 feet of you, makes a Strike for 2d6 force damage (with an attack bonus determined by the warhorn’s type), and then disappears. The warriors can [flank](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) with one another and with you and your allies.

Once the magic is used, the warhorn remains as a non‑magical musical instrument.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Talespinner's Lyre](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4660)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Olfactory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=246" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98

**Price** 235 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Plucking a _talespinner’s lyre_ while focusing on an event you witnessed causes the instrument to create an illusion in a 50-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) that plays out your memory of the event in real time, complete with sights, sounds, and smells. You can Sustain the Activation for up to 1 minute to keep it playing. The scene reproduces only what’s in its area, including nothing beyond that even if present in the memory. The scene is realistic, but all observers can clearly tell it’s an illusion. Observers can’t interact with the scene directly nor can they taste or touch elements of it to get a sensation you didn’t personally experience, but they can attempt skill checks to discern more about the scene without altering its contents. For example, no one could see something you didn’t, such as the true form of a creature [polymorphed](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) into a squirrel, but an observer might be able to use [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) and [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) to discern the squirrel was acting unlike a squirrel should.

Once the magic is used, the lyre remains as a non-magical virtuoso instrument.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Talespinner's Lyre

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact, Strike --- Carved of seashell, a _torrent spellgun_ is damp to the touch, and seaweed wraps around its grip. You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target’s AC. This spellgun has a range increment of 30 feet. The spellgun blasts a powerful jet of water that deals bludgeoning damage based on the spellgun’s type, then disintegrates into sand. **Critical Success** The target takes double damage and is knocked back 10 feet.
**Success** The target takes full damage and is knocked back 5 feet. Torrent Spellgun (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98 **Price** 12 gp --- The damage is 2d6. Torrent Spellgun (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98 **Price** 70 gp --- The damage is 7d6. Torrent Spellgun (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98 **Price** 275 gp --- The damage is 12d6. Torrent Spellgun (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98 **Price** 1,250 gp --- The damage is 16d6.
name: Torrent Spellgun

    level="1"

>
[Watchful Portrait](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4662)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=141" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 98

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _watchful portrait_ appears to be an innocuous depiction of a bored noble, unassuming relative, or unknown local celebrity. You Activate the portrait by hanging it. While it’s activated, you can use a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait to see through the eyes of the portrait instead of your own. You can do so while you’re within 500 feet of the portrait, even if it’s outside your line of sight or line of effect. You visually observe the area around the portrait from its perspective, using your own visual senses. While you’re scrying through it, the portrait’s eyes follow others more than usual. A creature that succeeds at a DC 30 Perception check notices this phenomenon. The scrying ends after 10 minutes or when you decide to stop watching through the portrait. 

The painting loses its magic when the scrying ends, if 4 hours pass after you Activate it without you looking through it, or if you Activate a different _watchful portrait_. The portrait then remains a normal, non-magical work of art, but due to its rather bland subject matter, it is worth no more than 5 gp.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Watchful Portrait

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 99 **Price** 50 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- A blue finish decorates the ceramic body of a _wind ocarina_. When you play a note on this ocarina, for 1 minute, winds sweep into strong gusts in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you. The winds have the following effects.
  • Ammunition from physical ranged attacks—such as arrows, bolts, sling bullets, and other objects of comparable size—can’t pass through the area. Passing through the area causes attacks with bigger ranged weapons, such as thrown weapons, to take a –2 circumstance penalty to their attack rolls if their paths pass through the emanation. Massive ranged weapons and spell effects that don’t create physical objects can pass through the emanation with no penalty.
  • The area is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) to creatures attempting to move through it.
  • Gases, including creatures in gaseous form, can’t pass through the emanation. When the emanation arises, such gases are removed from the area, including your space.
  • The area, including your space, has breathable air. Once the magic is used, the ocarina remains a non-magical virtuoso musical instrument.
name: Wind Ocarina

This large brass medallion hangs low on the torso. It’s shaped in the form of an unblinking eye, with a ring of turquoise as the iris and an orb of jet serving as the pupil. The amulet grants you a +2 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks. When you invest the amulet, you either increase your Wisdom score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727).

Each of these shining, bluish-gray metal armbands is adorned with the plated visage of a gorgon’s head. When targeted by a spell or effect with the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effect, you treat the result of your save as if it were one degree of success better, and the result of any check made to inflict such an effect on you as one degree of success worse (as if you were more than twice the level of the spell or effect targeting you). When you invest the armbands, you either increase your Constitution score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You clap the bracers together and remove a single condition of your choice currently afflicting you. If the condition is permanent, it’s instead suppressed for 1 hour.

</column>

These seemingly ordinary rectangular eyeglasses feature clear lenses framed in copper. When invested and worn, they rest perfectly on the bridge of the nose and can only be removed by the wearer. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks and any skill check made to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365). When you invest the spectacles, you either increase your Intelligence score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher. You must select the skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same artificer spectacles, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast a 3rd-level [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) spell on an item you touch.

While the insides of these boots are comfortable, fur-lined leather, the outsides are a jumble of slate plates, giving the impression of a rockslide. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and a +2 circumstance bonus to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) and [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380). When you invest the boots, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You succeed or critically succeed with a [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380);  **Effect** If the Shove was a success, you push your opponent up to 10 feet instead of 5 feet. If the Shove was a critical success, you push your opponent up to 20 feet, and you can then choose to knock them [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
